signals.headline: Rakuten Wallet Launches XRP as Payment Method | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Rakuten Wallet Launches XRP as Payment Method | YES | HIGH confidence**

On April 15, 2026, Rakuten Wallet went live with XRP as both a listed asset and payment method, reaching 44 million Rakuten Pay users and 5 million merchant locations[1]. This represents a major institutional adoption milestone, as Rakuten's 3 trillion loyalty points ($23 billion in circulation) can now convert to XRP[1].

signals.headline: Six Spot ETFs Now Active with ~$1B Combined AUM | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Six Spot ETFs Now Active with ~$1B Combined AUM | YES | HIGH confidence**

Six U.S. spot XRP ETFs are now live (Canary XRPC, Bitwise, Franklin Templeton, Grayscale, 21Shares, and Amplify) with approximately $1 billion in combined assets under management and around 787 million XRP locked in custody[1]. Week-ending April 11 saw inflows of $119.6 million, the strongest since December[1].

signals.headline: **Lakers Game 1 victory**: The Los Angeles Lakers defeated the Houston Rockets 107-98 in their first-round playoff match
signals.raw: **Lakers Game 1 victory**: The Los Angeles Lakers defeated the Houston Rockets 107-98 in their first-round playoff matchup, described as a "shock win."[2]

signals.headline: **LeBron James historic milestone**: LeBron became the **oldest player in NBA history to record a points/assists double-
signals.raw: **LeBron James historic milestone**: LeBron became the **oldest player in NBA history to record a points/assists double-double in a playoff game**, finishing with 19 points and 13 assists.[2] He also made history by playing alongside his son **Bronny James in the playoffs for the first time.**[2]

signals.headline: **Luke Kennard's breakout performance**: Luke Kennard led the Lakers with 27 points, the second-most in a Lakers postsea
signals.raw: **Luke Kennard's breakout performance**: Luke Kennard led the Lakers with 27 points, the second-most in a Lakers postseason debut, shooting 100% from three-point range on at least five attempts—joining only Robert Horry and Bryon Scott in Lakers playoff history.[2][5]

signals.headline: **Series outlook shifted**: Analysts have changed their predictions, with one commentator stating "I think the Lakers ar
signals.raw: **Series outlook shifted**: Analysts have changed their predictions, with one commentator stating "I think the Lakers are going to win the series" following the Game 1 victory.[1]

signals.headline: **Context**: The Rockets were without Kevin Durant due to injury, contributing to the upset result
signals.raw: **Context**: The Rockets were without Kevin Durant due to injury, contributing to the upset result.[2] The search results also confirm **Cleveland Cavaliers** took Game 1 over Toronto Raptors 126-113, with James Harden and Max Strus combining for 46 points.[2]
